advice: Keep skin clean and dry. Do not share bedding, towels, or clothes. If you a develop a rash while traveling: Avoid touching the infected area so it does not spread to other parts of your body. Consult a healthcare provider for treatment and testing. Avoid steroid creams, as they can make the ringworm worse. Use prescribed or over-the-counter (OTC) antifungals exactly as directed and contact a healthcare provider if treatment is not working. Topical antifungals and oral terbinafine are frequently ineffective against AR ringworm.

advice: Keep skin clean and dry. Do not share bedding, towels, or clothes. If you a develop a rash while traveling: Avoid touching the infected area so it does not spread to other parts of your body. Consult a healthcare provider for treatment and testing. Avoid steroid creams, as they can make the ringworm worse. Use prescribed or over-the-counter (OTC) antifungals exactly as directed and contact a healthcare provider if treatment is not working. Topical antifungals and oral terbinafine are frequently ineffective against AR ringworm.
